response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering credentials—MIT degree, 19+ years of experience, and staff-level seniority—this Principal Performance Architect role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and AI accelerator optimization. The resume shows exclusively software engineering experience (web, backend, microservices, mobile systems) with no evidence of hardware design, semiconductor knowledge, or AI accelerator workload analysis. Recent "AI-agent infrastructure" work refers to software frameworks, not hardware optimization.
strengths: MIT degree combined with 19+ years of engineering experience exceeds educational requirements (Bachelor's + 8+ years)
Demonstrated low-latency and performance optimization expertise through microservices architecture and DLang work at Dunnhumby
Staff-level seniority with proven ability to master multiple programming languages and technical domains
concerns: Zero experience with SoC design, IP modeling, or hardware architecture—the core responsibility of this principal role
No background in GPU optimization, AI accelerator design, or deep learning infrastructure workload analysis
Entire career is software-focused; lacks hardware-software co-design expertise and semiconductor domain knowledge essential for the position

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No GPU or AI accelerator optimization background
Staff-level software engineering seniority and breadth
